item.title: Is America a Christian nation? A conversation with Bishop Barron.
item.description: In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Adam O'Neal sits down with Bishop Robert Barron to discuss Christian nationalism, the role of Catholicism in America and whether Pope Leo is positioned as the "anti-Trump" pope.

item.title: Congress has a cruelty problem
item.description: The farm bill could erase state and local animal welfare laws.

item.title: He’s a devout Catholic. I’m an atheist novelist. Here’s how we stay married.
item.description: Interfaith marriage, in Christian, Jewish and atheist readers’ eyes

item.title: Trump deserves to have his prayers heard, too
item.description: Preaching, gerrymandering and teaching school, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: My generation had its unrealistic ‘love.’ Could this be worse?
item.description: Don’t let artificial intelligence teach young people about romance.

item.title: This railroad merger could give America a truly national system
item.description: The proposed merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern railroads is a case in point.

item.title: Phone addicts attend group therapy
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on phone addicts attending group therapy.

item.title: Why everyone is talking about peptides
item.description:
Peptides are suddenly everywhere — touted by influencers, biohackers and longevity enthusiasts as the next breakthrough in wellness. But what exactly are they, and how much do we actually know about the risks?
Host Megan McArdle talks with physician and Washington Post columnist Dr. Leana Wen about the booming peptide economy, the rise of gray-market injections and why so many people are turning to online health communities instead of doctors. As wellness culture pushes further into self-experimentation, how much risk are people willing to take in the pursuit of optimization?

item.title: Alaska’s good idea to empower pharmacists
item.description: States are increasingly expanding pharmacists’ ability to prescribe medications without a doctor.

item.title: Trump has devastated Iran’s regime. Don’t give it a lifeline now.
item.description: A decimated leadership is weakened, vulnerable and on the mat.

item.title: The ‘no’ in NATO
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, NATO.

item.title: The most abused fan base in basketball becomes a hope train
item.description: The Knicks might win a championship. There’s hope for us all.

item.title: Blocking the construction of data centers is a national security risk
item.description: Data infrastructure is critical to ensuring U.S. military strength.

item.title: Please take your Intel winnings, sir, and exit the casino
item.description: Fueled by a successful investment, the government is looking for opportunities.

item.title: A Hemingwayesque tale, with pencils and a Mad magazine twist
item.description: Gerry Gersten, a longtime contributor to the publication, died in 2017. His art lives on.

item.title: What I saw at the musclemaxxing ‘Enhanced Games’ in Vegas
item.description: The Enhanced Games promised a revolution. It fizzled.
